title: Ghost Mechanism: An Analytical Model of Abrupt Learning in Recurrent Networks
summary: This study establishes the ghost mechanism as an underlying mechanism for abrupt learning, whereby the recurrent neural network develops ghost points—transient dynamical bottlenecks—and identifies ways to overcome the training instabilities.

title: Passive Quantum Error Correction of Photon Loss at Breakeven
summary: Researchers achieve passive quantum error correction at the “breakeven” point. Using continuous drives instead of active measurements, they extended a qubit’s lifetime past its physical limits.

title: Stretching Theory of Hookean Metashells
summary: A continuum theory that describes how mechanical metamaterials deform on surfaces takes the form of the Schrödinger equation and provides a different path for materials design.

title: Stealthy-Hyperuniform Wave Dynamics in Two-Dimensional Photonic Crystals
summary: While disordered stealthy hyperuniform materials are expected to be largely transparent within a particular range of wavelengths, new experiments on large-scale silicon photonic crystals reveal unexpected residual scattering driven by radiative loss.

title: Ballistic Spin Valve in Graphene Realized via Electron Optics
summary: Transverse magnetic focusing in encapsulated graphene reveals gate-tunable ballistic spin transport, providing a mechanism for coherent spin control through spin-dependent electron optics.

title: Scaling Laws of Quantum Information Lifetime in Monitored Quantum Dynamics
summary: Researchers have discovered that recording data from midcircuit measurements can preserve quantum information for an exponentially long time. This finding offers a strategy to preserve fragile data and improve the performance of near-term quantum computers and algorithms.

title: Dipolar Nematic State in Relaxor Ferroelectrics
summary: A nematic ordering underpins the behavior of relaxor ferroelectrics.

title: Effect of Glass Stability on the Low Frequency Vibrations of Vapor Deposited Glasses
summary: An improved inelastic x-ray scattering technique helps clarify the connections between complex vibrations and the properties of glasses.
